summary: Frontend testing for bug fix: Totals sync between Määrälaskenta and Laskenta panels. All 5 tests passed. Bug fix verified working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix confirmed: TakeoffPanel.calculateRow() now correctly uses effectiveQuantity (which includes wallHeight × length - openings) for cost calculation
Both TakeoffPanel and CalculatorPanel use identical calculation logic: costQuantity * pricePerUnit
Wall measurement effectiveQuantity = (quantity * wallHeight * bothSidesFactor) - openings
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for totals sync between Määrälaskenta and Laskenta panels is verified working. Both panels now use consistent effectiveQuantity calculation for wall measurements.

tests_executed.details: Created wall measurement with Huoltomaalaus preset (10 €/m²). Totals matched: 137.91 € in both panels
Created rectangle measurement with Kipsikatto preset. Totals matched in both panels
Created count measurement with Door paint preset (90 €/kpl). Totals matched: 294.98 € in both panels

tests_executed.test: Edit wall height and openings - totals sync
tests_executed.details: After editing wall height and openings values, totals remained in sync between both panels
VAT 25.5% calculations identical in Määrälaskenta (173.07 €) and Laskenta (173.07 €) panels

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: Totals in right-side 'Määrälaskenta' panel may be out of sync with 'Laskenta' tab
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: TakeoffPanel's calculateRow function was using m.quantity directly for cost calculation instead of effectiveQuantity
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: calculateRow now uses effectiveQuantity (which includes wall height * length - openings calculation) for cost calculation
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: PASSED - All totals now match exactly between Määrälaskenta and Laskenta tabs
tested_scenarios: Wall measurement creation and totals comparison
Area measurement creation and totals comparison
Count measurement creation and totals comparison
Wall measurement edit (height/openings) and totals comparison

TakeoffPanel.js.calculateRow_function: Lines 256-288: Correctly calculates effectiveQuantity and costQuantity, then uses costQuantity * pricePerUnit for totalCost
TakeoffPanel.js.wall_calculation: For wall type: effectiveQuantity = (quantity * wallHeight * bothSidesFactor) - openings

CalculatorPanel.js.operationGroups_calculation: Lines 12-60: Uses identical calculation logic - costQuantity * pricePerUnit
CalculatorPanel.js.consistency: Both panels now produce matching totals for all measurement types
